questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A uniform cube of side a and mass m rests on a rough horizontal table. A horizontal force $\mathrm{F}$ is applied normal to one of the faces at a point that is directly above the centre of the face, at a height $3 \mathrm{a} / 4$ above the base. The minimum value of $\mathrm{F}$ for which the cube begins to topple an edge is (assume that cube does not slide)
questions.solution:
For toppling about edge $x$ x At the moment of toppling the normal force pass through axis $\mathrm{xx}$ '.
$$
\mathrm{F}_{\min } \frac{3 \mathrm{a}}{4}=\mathrm{mg} \frac{\mathrm{a}}{2} \text { or } \mathrm{F}_{\min }=\frac{2 \mathrm{mg}}{3}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A drum of radius $R$ and mass $M$ rolls down without slipping along an inclined plane of angle $\theta$. The frictional force:
options: decreases the rotational and translational motion
converts translational energy to rotational energy
questions.solution: Required frictional force converts translational energy into rotational energy.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Three particles, each of mass $m$ gram, are situated at the vertices of an equilateral triangle $\mathrm{ABC}$ side $l \mathrm{~cm}$ (as shown in the figure). The moment of inertia of the system about a line $\mathrm{AX}$ perpendicular to $A B$ and in the plane of $A B C$, in gram $\mathrm{cm}^2$ units will be:
questions.solution: According to the question $\begin{aligned} I_{A X} & =m l^2+m\left(\frac{l}{2}\right)^2 \\ & =m l^2+\frac{5}{4} \\ & =\frac{5}{4} m l^2 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If the time period is doubled, then the angular momentum of the body will (provided the moment of inertia of the body is constant)
questions.solution:
As we know.

L=Iω

⇒L=I×2πT

L∝1T

Given, T2=2T

Hence L1L2=2TT

⇒ L2=L12
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The moment of inertia of a thin uniform rod of mass $M$ and length $L$ about an axis passing through its mid-point and perpendicular to its length is $I_0$. Its moment of inertia about an axis passing through one of its ends and perpendicular to its length is
questions.solution:
The theorem of parallel axis for moment of inertia.
$\begin{aligned}
& I=I_{\mathrm{CM}}+M h^2 \\
& I=I_0+M\left(\frac{L}{2}\right)^2
\end{aligned}$
$I=I_0+\frac{M L^2}{4}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}$ is the force acting on a particle having position vector $\vec{r}$ and $\vec{\tau}$ be the torque of this force about the origin, then
questions.solution:
Torque is an axial vector i.e., its direction is always perpendicular to the plane containing vectors $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}$.
$\vec{\tau}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}$
Torque is perpendicular to both $\vec{r}$ and $\vec{F}$
$\therefore \quad \begin{aligned}
\vec{\tau} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}} & =0 \\
\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}} \cdot \vec{\tau} & =0
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A thin circular ring of mass $M$ and radius $R$ is rotating in a horizontal plane about an axis vertical to its plane with a constant angular velocity $\omega$. If two objects each of mass $m$ be attached gently to the opposite ends of a diameter of the ring, the ring will then rotate with an angular velocity
questions.solution:
Key Idea Apply law of conservation of angular momentum.
$\mathrm{I}_1 \omega_1=\mathrm{I}_2 \omega_2$
In the given case
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{I}_1 & =\mathrm{MR}^2 \\
\mathrm{I}_2 & =\mathrm{MR}^2+2 \mathrm{mR}^2 \\
\omega_1 & =\omega
\end{aligned}$
Then
$\omega_2=\frac{I_1}{I_2} \omega=\frac{M}{M+2 m} \omega$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The ratio of the accelerations for a solid sphere (mass 'm' and radius 'R') rolling down an incline of angle ' $\theta$ ' without slipping and slipping down the incline without rolling is:
questions.solution:
For solid sphere rolling without slipping on inclined plane, acceleration
$$
a_{1}=\frac{g \sin \theta}{1+\frac{K^{2}}{R^{2}}}
$$
For solid sphere slipping on inclined plane without rolling, acceleration
$$
a_{2}=g \sin \theta
$$
Therefore required ratio $=\frac{\mathrm{a}_{1}}{\mathrm{a}_{2}}$
$$
=\frac{1}{1+\frac{K^{2}}{R^{2}}}=\frac{1}{1+\frac{2}{5}}=\frac{5}{7}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A point P consider at the contact point of a wheel on the ground which roll on the ground without slipping, then the value of the displacement of point $\mathrm{P}$ when wheel completes half of the rotation (if radius of wheel is $1 \mathrm{~m}$ )is:
questions.solution: Displacement $P Q$ $\begin{aligned} & =\sqrt{(P M)^2+(Q M)^2} \\ & =\sqrt{(\pi R)^2+(2 R)^2} \\ & =\sqrt{\pi^2+4}\{\because R=1 \mathrm{~m}\} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two rings of radius $\mathrm{R}$ and $\mathrm{nR}$ made of same material have the ratio of moment of inertia about an axis passing through centre is 1: 8 . The value of $\mathrm{n}$ is
questions.solution:
Ratio of moment of inertia of the rings
$\frac{\mathrm{I}_{1}}{\mathrm{I}_{2}}=\left(\frac{\mathrm{M}_{1}}{\mathrm{M}_{2}}\right)\left(\frac{\mathrm{R}_{1}}{\mathrm{R}_{2}}\right)^{2}$
$\begin{aligned}=&\left(\frac{\lambda l_{1}}{\lambda l_{2}}\right)\left(\frac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}\right)^{2}=\left(\frac{2 \pi \mathrm{R}}{2 \pi \mathrm{n} \mathrm{R}}\right)\left(\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\mathrm{n} \mathrm{R}}\right)^{2} \\ &(\lambda=\text { linear density of wire }=\mathrm{constant}) \\ \Rightarrow \quad \frac{\mathrm{I}_{1}}{\mathrm{I}_{2}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{n}^{3}}=\frac{1}{8} \quad \text { (given) } \\ \therefore \quad \mathrm{n}^{3}=8 \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=2 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle of mass ' $m$ ' is projected with a velocity $v$ making an angle of $30^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. The magnitude of angular momentum of the projectile about the point of projection when the particle is at its maximum height ' $h$ ' is
questions.solution:
Angular momentum $L_{0}=p r_{\perp}$ $\left(\because\right.$ linear momentum $p=m v \cos \theta$ and $\left.\mathrm{r}_{1}=H\right)$

$\Rightarrow L_{0}=m v \cos \theta H$
$=m v \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \cdot \frac{v^{2} \sin ^{2} 30^{\circ}}{2 g}$
$=\frac{\sqrt{3} m v^{3}}{16 g}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $$ \text { A solid sphere of radius } R \text { has moment of inertia } J \text { about its qeometrical axis. If it is } $$ melted into a disc of radius $r$ and thickness $t$. If its moment of inertia about the tangential axis (which is perpendicular to plane of the disc), is also equal to $I$, then the value of $r$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\frac{2}{3} M R^2=\frac{1}{2} M r^2+M r^2$ or $\frac{2}{3} M R^2=\frac{3}{2} M r^2 \Rightarrow r=\frac{2}{\sqrt{15}} R$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A chain of mass m forming a circle of radius R is slipped on a smooth round cone with half-angle θ . Find the tension in the chain if it rotates with a constant angular velocity ω about a vertical axis coinciding with the symmetry axis of the cone.
questions.solution:
Every element of chain moves on a circular path. So, every element of the chain experiences centripetal force. We consider a small element Δm on the chain making angle Δθ at the centre





ΔN sin θ = Δmg  ...... (i)

Net force towards centre is

2 T sin Δ θ 2 - Δ N cos θ = Δ mR ω 2

Δ N⁡ cos θ = 2 T⁡ sin Δ θ 2 - Δ mR ω 2  .......... (ii)

From eqn.(i) and (ii) we get

cos θ = 2 T sin Δ θ 2 - Δ mR ω 2 Δ mg

But Δ θ  is very small.

∴   sin Δ θ 2 = Δ θ 2

∵   cot θ = T Δ θ - Δ mR ω 2 Δ mg

Here Δm makes an angle Δθ at the centre. The total mass of chain is m.

∴ The mass of chain per unit angle is

λ = m⁡ 2 π

∴   Δ m⁡ = λ Δ θ = m⁡ 2 π Δ θ = m⁡ Δ θ 2 π

∴   cot  θ= TΔθ− mΔθ 2π R ω 2 m 2π Δθ g

∴   T⁡ = R⁡ ω 2 + g cot θ m⁡ 2 π
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The moment of inertia of a rigid body in terms of its angular momentum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>L</mi></math> and kinetic energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
Angular momentum of a rigid body about a fixed axis is given by

L=Iω

Where I is moment of inertia and ω is angular velocity about that axis.

Kinetic energy of body is given by

K=12 Iω2

∴ K=12IIω2=L22I

⇒ I=L22K
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two discs of same moment of inertia <math> <mrow> <mrow><mo>(</mo> <mi>I</mi> <mo>)</mo></mrow></mrow> </math> are rotating in same sense about their regular axis passing through centre and perpendicular to the plane of disc with angular velocities <math><msub><mrow><mi>ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>. They are brought into contact face to face coinciding the axis of rotation. The expression for loss in energy during this process is
questions.solution:
The initial angular momentum of two discs is I1ω1+I2ω2=Iω1+ω2.

The final angular momentum of system is I1+I2ω=2Iω

From the conservation of angular momentum, ω=ω1+ω22.

Initial kinetic energy, KEi=12Iω12+ω22 

and final kinetic energy, KEf=122Iω1+ω224

Loss in kinetic energy is ΔKE=12I1I2I1+I2 ω1-ω22
=12I22I ω1-ω22
=14Iω1-ω22
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The y– coordinate of centre of mass of a thin and uniform semicircular ring of radius r shown in figure is
questions.solution:
The wire is uniform. So, the mass per unit length of the wire μ=Mπr.

Let us assume a small element on the semicircular ring as shown in the figure.

Mass of the element dm=Mπrrdθ=Mπdθ

The y-coordinate of the centre of mass

ycm=1M∫ydm⇒ ycm=1M∫0πrsinθMπdθ⇒ ycm=rπ1+1⇒ ycm=2rπ
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two masses are attached to a rod end to end. If torque is applied they rotate with angular acceleration $\alpha$. If their distances are doubled and same torque is applied, then they move with angular acceleration
questions.solution: Suppose the masses $m_1$ and $m_2$ are attached to the ends of a massless rod. $C_1$ is the axis of rotation which is at a distance $R$ from each of the masses. $\therefore$ the moment of inertia of the system, $$ \mathrm{I}=m_1 R^2+m_2 R^2=\left(m_1+m_2\right) R^2 $$ Now if the distance between the masses is doubled, then the moment of inertia $$ \Gamma=m_1(2 R)^2+m_2(2 R)^2=\left(m_1+m_2\right) 4 R^2 $$ Since the same torque is applied in both the cases, $$ \begin{aligned} & \tau=I \alpha=I^{\prime} \alpha^{\prime} \\ & \Rightarrow \alpha^{\prime}=\frac{I \alpha}{I^{\prime}}=\frac{\left(m_1+m_2\right) R^2 \alpha}{\left(m_1+m_2\right) 4 R^2}=\frac{\alpha}{4} \end{aligned} $$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
